event_log.detail: Keep a glass of water within reach.
event_log.title: Drink water

event_log.detail:
What: Britain's Calder Hall became the world's first nuclear power station to deliver electricity on an industrial scale.
Why it matters: It moved nuclear power from laboratory demonstration to genuine commercial output.
Why you should care: It began the fleet of large reactors that today supply roughly a tenth of the world's electricity.
(Source: World Nuclear Association)
event_log.title: ⚡ Today's spark — Calder Hall, first industrial-scale nuclear (1956)
